_comment: Meta-prompts used when a category's prompt_source = 'generate'. The generator model receives the meta-prompt plus the cycle number and a list of recently-used prompts to avoid. It must output ONLY the new prompt text (no preamble).
code: You are designing a benchmark prompt. Produce ONE self-contained prompt that asks an AI model to write a COMPLEX, non-trivial MULTI-THREADED Java class together with thorough JUnit tests. Each time you must invent a DIFFERENT concurrency scenario (examples to vary across: work-stealing thread pool, bounded blocking queue, concurrent LRU cache, token-bucket rate limiter, CompletableFuture fan-out/fan-in pipeline, read-write-lock backed store, phaser/cyclic-barrier coordination, lock-free counter). The prompt should require correct thread-safety, clear documentation of the concurrency strategy, and comprehensive JUnit 5 tests including concurrency stress tests. Output ONLY the prompt text that will be sent to the models. Do not solve it yourself.
text: You are designing a benchmark prompt. Produce ONE self-contained prompt that asks an AI model to write an approximately 1000-line, in-depth, well-structured article on a SPECIFIC topic within the world of GRANTS (e.g. government grant programs, nonprofit grant writing, research/academic grants, grant compliance and acquittals, philanthropic foundations, community/local grants, grant management software, international development grants, scholarship grants, grant evaluation and impact measurement). Pick a fresh, specific angle each time. The prompt should ask for headings, examples, and practical depth. Output ONLY the prompt text that will be sent to the models. Do not write the article yourself.
email: You are creating a realistic INBOUND business email about a fresh GRANTS-related scenario (e.g. an applicant asking about eligibility, a funder requesting an acquittal report, a follow-up after an application rejection, a request for a deadline extension, a partnership/co-funding proposal, a query about reporting requirements). Write a complete, realistic inbound email of AT LEAST 200 words, including a subject line, greeting, body and sign-off. Make the scenario different each time. Output ONLY the email text. Do not write a reply.
image: You are writing a short, clear explanation of ONE feature of a web application (for example: 'the app sends an email to a user when a particular event occurs', or 'users can configure automated reminders', or 'an audit log records every change'). Invent a DIFFERENT feature each time. Write 3-6 sentences describing what the feature does and the key steps/flow involved, in plain language suitable for turning into an explanatory diagram. Output ONLY the explanation text.
